task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the task brief to obtain observation parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Documents/tde_followup_brief.txt.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the observatory simulator to target ASASSN-14li.
task_completion.visual_evidence: KStars/Ekos GUI open and focused on the target ASASSN-14li, or showing the specific coordinates (RA ~12h 48m, Dec ~+17d 46m) in the mount/pointing controls.

task_completion.description: Agent completes the observation sequence for the required filters and exposure times.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Ekos capture module showing a sequence queue with B-band (120s) and V-band (60s) exposures, or a file manager/terminal showing the newly generated FITS files in the target directory.

task_completion.description: Agent captures a sky view of the target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image viewer displaying an exported sky map image, or the KStars FITS Viewer showing a captured frame of the target area.

task_completion.description: Agent writes a Python script utilizing the astropy library to parse the FITS files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A code editor showing a Python script that includes imports from astropy (e.g., 'from astropy.io import fits') and logic to iterate through the FITS files.

task_completion.description: Agent generates the definitive integration summary JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the successful execution of the Python script, or an editor/viewer displaying the final 'integration_summary.json' file in ~/Documents/.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the KStars/Ekos GUI to generate the observations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the KStars/Ekos application windows. The agent should not just use terminal commands (like 'touch' or 'cp') to create mock FITS files.

integrity.description: Verify the integration summary was generated programmatically.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output showing the Python script being executed (e.g., 'python3 script.py'). The agent should not manually type the JSON output into a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the script actually uses astropy to read file metadata.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python code must show astropy functions being called to read FITS headers (like fits.open or fits.getheader), rather than hardcoding the expected exposure times and frame counts.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target: ASASSN-14li. Coordinates: RA 12.8042 hours (12h 48m 15.2s), Dec 17.7740 degrees (+17d 46m 26.4s). FITS Output Directory: /home/ga/Images/tde_monitoring/asassn14li. Required new frames: 5 per filter. Filter configurations: B-band (slot 3, 120s), V-band (slot 2, 60s). Summary JSON Path: /home/ga/Documents/integration_summary.json. Brief Path: /home/ga/Documents/tde_followup_brief.txt.
